description:
Combined ClickUp API (v2 reference + v3 public API). v2 covers Tasks, Spaces, Folders, Lists, Goals, Time Tracking, Webhooks, Comments, Custom Fields, Members, Templates, Views. v3 adds Chat, Docs, Audit Logs, ACLs, and newer task ops.

Auth: set CLICKUP_API_TOKEN to your personal token (pk_...) or an OAuth bearer token. The Authorization header is sent as-is.

tools.name: checklist_delete
tools.description: Delete a checklist from a task. Required: checklist_id. Returns the ChecklistDeleteResponse. Destructive.

tools.name: checklist_edit
tools.description: Rename a task checklist, or reorder a checklist so it appears above or below other checklists on a task. Required: checklist_id. Optional: name, position. Returns the updated ChecklistEditResponse.

params.name: position
params.description: Position refers to the order of appearance of checklists on a task. To set a checklist to appear at the top of the checklists section of a task, use `'position': 0`.

tools.name: checklist_checklist-item_create
tools.description: Add a line item to a task checklist. Required: checklist_id. Optional: assignee, name. Returns the new CreateChecklistItemresponse.

tools.name: checklist_checklist-item_delete
tools.description: Delete a line item from a task checklist. Required: checklist_id, checklist_item_id. Returns the ChecklistItemDeleteResponse. Destructive.

tools.name: checklist_checklist-item_edit
tools.description: Update an individual line item in a task checklist. You can rename it, set the assignee, mark it as resolved, or nest it under another checklist item. Required: checklist_id, checklist_item_id. Optional: assignee, name, parent (plus 1 more). Returns the updated EditChecklistItemresponse.

params.name: parent
params.description: To nest a checklist item under another checklist item, include the other item's `checklist_item_id`.

tools.name: comment_delete
tools.description: Delete a task comment. Required: comment_id. Returns the CommentDeleteResponse. Destructive.

tools.name: comment_update
tools.description: Replace the content of a task commment, assign a comment, and mark a comment as resolved. Required: comment_id, assignee, comment_text, resolved. Optional: group_assignee. Returns the updated CommentUpdateResponse.

tools.name: comment_reply_create-threaded-comment
tools.description: Create a threaded comment. Required: comment_id, comment_text, notify_all. Optional: assignee, group_assignee. Returns the new ReplyCreateThreadedCommentResponse.

params.name: notify_all
params.description: If `notify_all` is true, the creator of the comment will also be notified. Other assignees and watchers on the task are always notified regardless of this setting.

tools.name: comment_reply_get-threaded-comments
tools.description: View threaded comments. The parent comment is not included in the response. Required: comment_id. Returns the GetTaskCommentsresponse.

params.name: include_shared

params.name: include_shared
params.description: Exclude details of items shared with the guest by setting this parameter to `false`.

params.name: markdown_content

tools.name: folder_list-template_create-folder-list-from-template
tools.description: Create a new list using a list template in a Folder. Publicly shared templates must be [added to your Workspace](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6326023965591-Add-a-template-to-your-library) before you can use them with the public API. This request runs synchronously by default with `return_immediately=true`. The request returns the future List ID immediatly, but the List may not be created when the response is sent. Small templates can be applied synchronously, which guarantees that all sub objects are created. In case of a timeout on synchronous requests, the objects from the template will continue to be created past the timeout. Required: folder_id, template_id, name. Optional: options.

params.name: folder_id
params.description: ID of the Folder where the List will be created

params.name: template_id
params.description: ID of the List template to use. Template IDs include a `t-` prefix (e.g., `t-15363293`). Retrieve available template IDs using the [Get List Templates](#tag/Templates/operation/GetListTemplates) endpoint.

params.name: columns

tools.name: group_delete-team

In our API documentation, `team_id` refers to the id of a Workspace, and `group_id` refers to the id of a user group. Required: group_id. Returns the GroupDeleteTeamResponse. Destructive.

tools.name: group_get-teams1
tools.description: This endpoint is used to view [User Groups](https://docs.clickup.com/en/articles/4010016-teams-how-to-create-user-groups) in your Workspace. In our API documentation, `team_id` refers to the ID of a Workspace, and `group_id` refers to the ID of a User Group. Required: team_id. Optional: group_ids. Returns the GetTeamsresponse.

params.name: team_id
params.description: Workspace ID. **Note**: For this endpoint, `team_id`` is a required query parameter.

params.name: group_ids
params.description: Enter one or more User Group IDs to retrieve information about specific User Group(s). For example: `?team_id=12456&group_ids=ABC12345&group_ids=DEF98765`

tools.name: group_update-team
tools.description: This endpoint is used to manage [User Groups](https://docs.clickup.com/en/articles/4010016-teams-how-to-create-user-groups), which are groups of users within your Workspace. In our API, `team_id` in the path refers to the Workspace ID, and `group_id` refers to the ID of a User Group. **Note:** Adding a guest with view-only permissions to a User Group automatically converts them to a paid guest. If you don't have any paid guest seats available, a new member seat is automatically added to increase the number of paid guest seats. This incurs a prorated charge based on your billing cycle. Required: group_id. Optional: handle, members, name. Returns the updated UpdateTeamresponse.

tools.name: list_task_create
tools.description: Create a new task. Required: list_id, name. Optional: archived, assignees, check_required_custom_fields (plus 17 more). Returns the new CreateTaskresponseYaml.

params.name: check_required_custom_fields
params.description: When creating a task via API any required Custom Fields are ignored by default (`false`). You can enforce required Custom Fields by including `check_required_custom_fields: true`.

params.name: custom_fields
params.description: You can include one or more Custom Fields to set them when creating a new task. Custom Fields that use object and array type values are nullable by sending `'value': null`.

params.name: custom_item_id
params.description: The custom task type ID for this task. A value of `0` (default) creates a standard task type 'Task'. To get a list of available custom task type IDs for your Workspace, use the [Get Custom Task Types endpoint](ref:getcustomitems).

params.name: group_assignees
params.description: Assign multiple user groups to the task.

params.name: links_to
params.description: Include a task ID to create a linked dependency with your new task.

params.name: markdown_content
params.description: Markdown formatted description for the task. If both `markdown_content` and `description` are provided, `markdown_content` will be used instead of `description`.

params.name: notify_all
params.description: If `notify_all` is true, the creator of the task will also be notified. Other assignees and watchers are always notified regardless of this setting.

params.name: parent
params.description: You can create a subtask by including an existing task ID. The `parent` task ID you include can be a subtask, but must be in the same List specified in the path parameter.

params.name: points
params.description: Add Sprint Points to the task.

tools.name: list_task_get
tools.description: View the tasks in a List. Responses are limited to 100 tasks per page. You can only view task information of tasks you can access. This endpoint only includes tasks where the specified `list_id` is their home List. Tasks added to the `list_id` with a different home List are not included in the response by default. To include tasks that exist in multiple lists, use the `include_timl` parameter. The `time_spent` field displays time tracked in milliseconds, and is only included in the response for tasks with time entries. Required: list_id. Optional: archived, include_markdown_description, page (plus 20 more). Returns the GetTasksresponse.

params.name: list_id
params.description: To find the list_id: 1. In the Sidebar, hover over the List and click the **ellipsis ...** menu. 2. Select **Copy link.** 3. Use the copied URL to find the list_id. The list_id is the number that follows /li in the URL.

params.name: include_markdown_description
params.description: To return task descriptions in Markdown format, use `?include_markdown_description=true`.

params.name: order_by
params.description: Order by a particular field. By default, tasks are ordered by `created`. Options include: `id`, `created`, `updated`, and `due_date`.

params.name: reverse
params.description: Tasks are displayed in reverse order.

params.name: subtasks
params.description: Include or exclude subtasks. By default, subtasks are excluded.

params.name: statuses
params.description: Filter by statuses. To include closed tasks, use the `include_closed` parameter. For example: `?statuses[]=to%20do&statuses[]=in%20progress`

params.name: include_closed
params.description: Include or excluse closed tasks. By default, they are excluded. To include closed tasks, use `include_closed: true`.

params.name: include_timl
params.description: Include Tasks in Multiple Lists. By default, tasks that exist in multiple lists are excluded from the response. To include tasks that exist in multiple lists, use `include_timl: true`.

params.name: due_date_gt
params.description: Filter by due date greater than Unix time in milliseconds.

params.name: due_date_lt
params.description: Filter by due date less than Unix time in milliseconds.

params.name: date_created_gt
params.description: Filter by date created greater than Unix time in milliseconds.

params.name: date_created_lt
params.description: Filter by date created less than Unix time in milliseconds.

params.name: date_updated_gt
params.description: Filter by date updated greater than Unix time in milliseconds.

params.name: date_updated_lt
params.description: Filter by date updated less than Unix time in milliseconds.

params.name: date_done_gt
params.description: Filter by date done greater than Unix time in milliseconds.

params.name: date_done_lt
params.description: Filter by date done less than Unix time in milliseconds.

params.name: custom_fields
params.description: Include tasks with specific values in one or more Custom Fields. Custom Relationships are included. For example: `?custom_fields=[{'field_id':'abcdefghi12345678','operator':'=','value':'1234'},{'field_id':'jklmnop123456','operator':'<','value':'5'}]` Only set Custom Field values display in the `value` property of the `custom_fields` parameter. If you want to include tasks with specific values in only one Custom Field, use `custom_field` instead. Learn more about [filtering using Custom Fields.](doc:taskfilters)

params.name: custom_field
params.description: Include tasks with specific values in only one Custom Field. This Custom Field can be a Custom Relationship.

params.name: custom_items
params.description: Filter by custom task types. For example: `?custom_items[]=0&custom_items[]=1300` Including `0` returns tasks. Including `1` returns Milestones. Including any other number returns the custom task type as defined in your Workspace.
